description: Fix fetchJSON code examples across documentation to handle the T | undefined return type. The 2-arg fetchJSON<T>(url, init?) overload returns Promise<T | undefined> because of 204 No Content handling, but multiple doc examples access properties on the result without null-checking. Under the scaffolded plugin tsconfig (strict: true), copying these examples produces TS18048 errors. Each affected file needs its fetchJSON examples updated to either use the 3-arg Zod schema overload (which returns T, not T | undefined) or add explicit null-checks before accessing properties.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix fetchJSON examples in resources-prompts guide
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the resources-prompts guide code examples to compile without type errors so that I can copy them into my plugin and have them work under strict TypeScript.
acceptanceCriteria: All fetchJSON calls in the 'Full Plugin Example' code block (lines ~429-509) use either the 3-arg Zod schema overload OR have explicit null-checks before property access
The 'Typed Resources with Schema' example (line ~137) handles the undefined case
The 'Typed Prompts with Zod Schema' example (lines ~348-349) handles the undefined case
Code examples remain clear and readable for first-time plugin developers

cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx

There are 5 instances to fix in this file:

1. Lines 137-138: `const data = await fetchJSON<{...}>('/api/channels')` then `JSON.stringify(data)` — data could be undefined.
2. Lines 348-349: `const data = await fetchJSON<{...}>('/api/items')` then `data.items.map(...)` — no null check.
3. Lines 435-440: `const data = await fetchJSON<{...}>('/api/items')` then `data.items` — no null check.
4. Lines 454-458: Same pattern as #3 in the prompt's render function.
5. Lines 487-492: `const item = await fetchJSON<{...}>('/api/items', { method: 'POST', ... })` then `item.id`, `item.title` — no null check, plus returning `item` where the output schema expects a concrete object.

The recommended fix is to add a null-check after each fetchJSON call: `if (!data) throw ToolError.internal('...')` for tools, or `if (!data) return { uri, text: '[]', mimeType: 'application/json' }` for resources. This pattern is already used correctly in sdk/resources.mdx (lines 183-189) and sdk/prompts.mdx (lines 221-227) — follow that established pattern.

Alternatively, use the 3-arg Zod schema overload where appropriate: `fetchJSON('/api/items', undefined, itemsSchema)` which returns T (not T | undefined) and throws ToolError.validation on parse failure. This is the safer pattern but adds complexity to introductory examples.

The simplest fix is adding `if (!data) { throw ToolError.internal('No data returned'); }` after each 2-arg fetchJSON call. Make sure to import ToolError if it's not already imported in the code block.
